step_rationale: Plasmin generation marker; reflects fibrinolytic activity and endogenous thrombus breakdown.
mechanism: Plasmin-antiplasmin complexes quantify active fibrinolysis of the coronary thrombus, indicating both thrombus formation and the fibrinolytic response in acute MI.

references.title: Microparticle-linked tissue factor activity and increased thrombin activity play a potential role in fibrinolysis failure in ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction.
Absence of paradoxical thrombin activation by fibrin-specific thrombolytics in acute myocardial infarction: comparison of single-bolus tenecteplase and front-loaded alteplase.

Fibrin specificity and procoagulant effect related to the kallikrein-contact phase system and to plasmin generation with double-bolus reteplase and front-loaded alteplase thrombolysis in acute myocardial infarction.
